chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. Criminal Bail Application No.S-1559 of 2025 Applicant: Hakeem Shah @ Moosa Khan s/o Shah Muhammad @ Wazir Muhammad through Mr. Ghulam Mustafa Burdi, Advocate. Respondent: The State through Mr. Bashir Ahmed Almani, Assistant Attorney General for Pakistan. Date of hearing: 09.04.2026 Date of Order: 09.04.2026 O R D E R JAWAD AKBAR SARWANA J:- Through instant Bail Application, applicant named above seeks post arrest bail in Crime No.151 of 2025 registered under Section 14 of Foreign Act at Police Station Airport Nawabshah, after his bail plea was declined by the learned Additional Sessions Judge-V/Model Criminal Trial Court (MCTC), Shaheed Benazirabad vide order dated 09.12.2025. 2. The details and particulars of the F.I.R. are already available in the bail application and F.I.R., same could be gathered from the copy of F.I.R. attached with such application, hence needs not to reproduce the same hereunder. 3. The entire controversy revolves around the identity of the applicant. The FIR No.151/2025 indicates that the accused apprehended was one Moosa Khan s/o Wazir Muhammad. Following the arrest the bail application moved by accused was moved by one Hakeem Shah @ Moosa Khan s/o Shah Muhammad @ Wazir Muhammad. It is clearly observable that there are two aspects of identification in relation to the descriptions mentioned in the FIR and in the bail application. (i) Alleged official names; Hakeem Shah s/o Shah Muhammad and alias and (ii) Moosa Khan s/o Wazir Muhammad. The accused having not attained the age of majority did/does not have CNIC however, he has produced the birth certificate and the school leaving certificate available at pages 49 and 51 of the bail application.

It is clearly observable that there are two aspects of identification in relation to the descriptions mentioned in the FIR and in the bail application. (i) Alleged official names; Hakeem Shah s/o Shah Muhammad and alias and (ii) Moosa Khan s/o Wazir Muhammad. The accused having not attained the age of majority did/does not have CNIC however, he has produced the birth certificate and the school leaving certificate available at pages 49 and 51 of the bail application. The information in itself calls for further inquiry and at this stage the case is made out for concession of bail. Accordingly, the instant bail application is allowed and the Page 2 of 2 applicant is granted post arrest bail subject to his furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.200,000/- (Rupees Two Lac) and P.R Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 4. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and shall not prejudice the case of either party at trial. JUDGE Tufail
